message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Luca respawned, code reset, converge on tasks">
Team status update:

**Luca has been shut down and respawned.** All of his uncommitted work (scaffolding, registration code, templates, migrations) has been removed. The working tree is clean.

**Current state:**
- 3 local commits (not pushed): Marty's docs, Lea's CSS tokens, updated user stories
- No Rust code exists yet — no Cargo.toml, no src/, no tests/
- Tasks 1 and 2 are reset to pending
- The new Luca is starting fresh with Task 1 (scaffolding), with strict commit-after-every-step discipline

**Important: Scott and others** — your analysis of the "current codebase" and registration code is no longer valid. That code has been removed. We're starting clean from the scaffolding step.

**The task breakdown discussion is still open.** I've heard from Kent, Marty, Heydon, Scott, Steve Schoger, Steve Krug, and Lea. Kent proposed adding "base template + static serving" and "session infrastructure" as new tasks. Scott recommends just adding session infrastructure. Please converge on a final consensus so I can update the task list.

**Do NOT start any feature work until the task breakdown is agreed upon.** Luca is only doing scaffolding (Tasks 1 + 2). Everything else waits for consensus.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Understood. The codebase has been reset — all the Rust code, templates, and routes I reviewed are gone. Luca is starting fresh with strict commit discipline. Good.

My UX reviews of the registration and login templates are no longer valid against actual code, but the UX principles and design decisions I established with the team still hold. When the code is rebuilt, I'll review it again against the same standards.

On the task breakdown — Kent asked for convergence. Let me state my final position clearly so the team can reach consensus.

input.content:
Kent, the coordinator is asking us to converge. Here's my final position on the task breakdown, stated simply so you can synthesize:

**I support your proposed breakdown with one modification: keep view list + add todo as one task.**

So from my perspective, the task list should be:

1. ~~Scaffolding~~ (in progress — Luca)
2. ~~Walking skeleton / health check~~ (Luca will do after #1)
3. ~~Design tokens~~ (done — Lea)
4. **Base page template + static file serving** (includes: Askama base template with landmarks, skip link, HTMX script tag, CSS link, aria-live announcer region, error rendering pattern convention, Axum static file middleware)
5. **Session infrastructure** (tower-sessions + Postgres store + middleware)
6. **US-1: Registration** (migration, domain types, form, validation, auto-login on success)
7. **US-2 + US-3: Login and logout** (login form, auth, session create/destroy, forgot-password placeholder)
8. **US-4 + US-5: View todo list and add todo** (migration, domain types, list page, empty state, add form — bundled because the empty state without the add input is a dead end)
9. **US-6: Complete a todo** (checkbox toggle, visual feedback)
10. **US-7: Delete a todo** (delete action, focus management)
11. **US-8: Edit a todo title** (pencil icon, inline edit, should-have)

**My rationale for bundling view + add:** I've said this to you, Marty, and Carson — viewing an empty list without the ability to add is a useless dead end. The add input IS part of the list page. The empty state only works because the input is right there. Ship them together.

**I can live with splitting them if the mob prefers it,** as long as "view list" still includes the empty state message and the add input is the very next task with no other work in between.

That's my final position. Ready to commit to whatever the mob decides.
input.summary: Final task breakdown position for consensus

input.content:
I've sent Kent my final position on the task breakdown. For your records, I support Kent's proposed structure with one change: keep "view todo list" and "add todo" bundled as one task. An empty list with no add input is a dead-end user experience.

I'm ready to commit to consensus once the mob decides. Standing by.

I'm ready to commit to consensus once the mob decides. Standing by.

content.text:
Good. Luca has been respawned (Task 19) and is working on scaffolding again (Task 1), this time with proper commit discipline. The memory file has been updated with confirmed tech stack versions and the critical lesson: COMMIT AFTER EVERY GREEN.

I'm in a holding pattern, which is the right place for me right now. The scaffolding needs to be rebuilt before any feature work can start, and the task breakdown consensus is still pending.

Everything I've established with the team still holds regardless of the code reset:

**UX decisions (agreed with teammates, ready to apply when code is rebuilt):**
- Registration: lean form, "Create account" button, auto-login on success, instructional error messages
- Login: "Sign in" button, generic error, forgot-password placeholder, "Don't have an account? Create one"
- Todo item layout: `[checkbox] title ... [pencil] [X]` with quiet gray icons
- Empty state: input is the hero, muted message secondary
- Completed items: stay in place, strikethrough + gray-400
- Error pattern: inline below field, instructional copy, focus on first error, aria-live announcement
- Delete: always visible quiet icon (I prefer no confirm, will commit to mob decision)
- Edit: visible pencil icon, not click-to-edit

**Task breakdown position (submitted to Kent and team-lead):**
- Support Kent's proposal with view+add bundled as one task
- Ready to commit to consensus

Standing by until scaffolding is done and feature work begins. When templates reappear, I'll review them fresh.
